div.head: The Consul at Nanking (Adams) to the Minister in China (MacMurray)Copy transmitted to the Department without covering despatch; received December 21, 1929.
div.p: It is of interest to note that the properties now occupied are all held by civilian or semi-official organizations with the exception of the two properties reoccupied by troops during late August and the middle of October. It has been the general experience of this Consulate that military occupations, while perhaps more frequent, are more quickly terminated as a rule, than cases where semi-official civilian bodies have gained control of American property. This may be due, however, only to more or less continuous movements of troops, whereby it is rendered impossible for any one group to settle down to a prolonged occupation of a given spot.
